programs.title: Big Fish
programs.desc: Edward Bloom (Albert Finney), a brilliant storyteller and favorite of the neighborhood, is dying.His son Will (Billy Crudup) left home long ago, not wanting to live forever in the shadow of his father's fantastic stories.Now she returns to accompany him in his last moments.Listening to Edward's stories again, Will tries to put them together to make sense.The picture that emerges is surprisingly coherent and shows the father's life in a new light.The son realizes who his father really was.Tim Burton, one of the most eccentric creators of American cinema, created a film that captivates with its warmth, fairy-tale atmosphere and surreal poetics.The script is based on the novel "Big Fish: A Story of Mythic Proportions" by Daniel Wallace.We will see Ewan McGregor as young Edward.The atmospheric music received an Oscar nomination.

programs.title: Detective Monk: One last thing
programs.desc: Brilliant detective Adrian Monk (Tony Shalhoub) is going through difficult times.The contract to publish his autobiography was canceled, and the coronavirus pandemic intensified his fears and phobias.All this makes the master of deduction start thinking about ending his life and joining his beloved Truda.He is jolted out of his torpor by the tragedy that befalls his stepdaughter Molly (Caitlin McGee).The girl is getting ready to marry journalist Griffin Briggs (Austin Scott).However, her fiancé dies while bungee jumping.Molly is convinced that it was not an accident, but a carefully planned murder.The trail leads to the influential billionaire Rick Eden (James Purefoy).Retired Captain Stottlemeyer (Ted Levine) now works for the rich man as his security chief.Monk is assisted in the investigation by former assistant and now real estate agent Natalie Teeger (Traylor Howard) and Randy Disher (Jason Gray-Stanford), now the New Jersey police commissioner.Continuation of the cult series.

programs.title: In-laws 3
programs.desc: In a recreation center that has already passed its glory days, Wanda (Izabela Kuna) and Tadeusz (Adam Woronowicz) organize their grandson's baptism.At the family celebration, to which they invite Małgorzata (Maja Ostaszewska) and her friend, psychotherapist Grażyna (Magdalena Popławska), the former husband of the first woman, Andrzej (Marcin Dorociński), who comes from Australia with... his son Stanley (Noah Lucewicz), also appears.The atmosphere of the meeting quickly gets out of control, and old conflicts and new romances lead to surprising consequences.The local police must intervene.The third part of the comedy, with a pinch of salt, showing the class struggle.

programs.title: One of Them Days
programs.desc: Dreux (Keke Palmer) and Alyssa (SZA) are like lovebirds, they have been friends for years and share an apartment.Their everyday lives suddenly turn upside down when they discover that Alyssa's boyfriend has squandered all their savings - money that was supposed to be used to pay the rent.The girls face a real challenge: in an atmosphere full of funny adventures, they must quickly find a way to get the missing amount and avoid eviction.It quickly turns out that they will be forced to take truly unusual actions to not only save their home, but also put their friendship to the test.

programs.title: Another Simple Favor
programs.desc: Emily Nelson (Blake Lively), who was sentenced to 20 years in prison, regains her freedom.She is planning a wedding on the island of Capri, and an old friend becomes her bridesmaid.A crime occurs during the ceremony.

programs.title: Welcome to Marwen
programs.desc: Mark Hogancamp (Steve Carell) is an illustrator, known primarily for his World War II graphics.However, he hides quite an original preference.He is fascinated by women's shoes.At home, she has a large collection of high heels, sandals, flip-flops and many other shoes.His secret is revealed unexpectedly and causes great emotions.He is attacked by a group of neo-Nazis.The injuries are so severe that they result in loss of not only memory, but also the ability to speak and move.His artistic career is ruined.The man starts therapy, hoping to regain his former shape.However, he struggles with enormous trauma and mental problems.He finally manages to find a new passion.He constructs a village in the garden, which he names Marwen.Its inhabitants, the dolls, are modeled on real people from his life.It turns out that the mockup helps him deal with panic attacks.However, he still cannot forget his experiences.Meanwhile, a new neighbor, Nicol (Leslie Mann), moves in next door.The film is inspired by the true story of artist Mark Hogancamp.The town he built is located in Kingston.
